subclassFeature.name: Nightstalker
entries: Nightstalkers are the ruthless executioners of the darkest pantheons. Whether fueled by the strife of Cyric, the twisting influence of Mask, or the festering hate of Moander, the Nightstalker is a walking herald of the "Crawling Abyss." They are not mere murderers; they are zealous instruments of the unseen. Striking from the ceiling or erupting from the floorboards, they drag their blades through the choking dark to deliver the perfect, terrifying blow.

subclassFeature.name: Shroud of the Deceiver
entries: You have been initiated into the arts of the unseen. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Gloom Sight
entries: You gain Darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have Darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet. You can see normally through areas of heavy obscurity caused by smoke, fog, or magical darkness created by your own spells or features.

entries.name: Vicious Sneak Attack
entries: Your strikes are lethal when unseen. When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature while you are Invisible, in Darkness, or in an area of Heavy Obscurity, you roll {@dice d8}s instead of {@dice d6}s for your Sneak Attack damage.

subclassFeature.name: Cunning Strike: Choking Cloud
entries: You gain a new option for your Cunning Strike:

entries.name: Choking Cloud (Cost: 1d6)
entries: You shatter a vial of caustic soot. A 10-foot-radius sphere of gray smoke appears centered on the target. The sphere spreads around corners, and its area is Heavily Obscured. The smoke dissipates at the start of your next turn or if dispersed by a moderate or stronger wind. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 + your Dexterity modifier + your Proficiency Bonus) or be {@condition Blinded} until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Shadow Step
entries: At 9th level, you gain the ability to step from one shadow into another. When you are in dim light or darkness, you can use a Bonus Action to teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see that is also in dim light or darkness. You have Advantage on the first melee attack you make before the end of the turn.

entries.name: Shadow Sprint
entries: If you use your Cunning Action to Dash or Disengage, you can weave this teleport into that movement, allowing you to teleport as part of that same Bonus Action.

subclassFeature.name: Lingering Hate
entries: The influence of your deity seeps into your blades, leaving a supernatural rot. When you deal Sneak Attack damage to a creature, you can curse it with lingering hate. The curse lasts for 1 minute or until the target dies.

entries.name: Hexed Blade
entries: While cursed, the target takes an extra {@damage 1d6} necrotic damage whenever you hit it with an attack.

entries.name: Cursed Mind/Body
entries: When you apply the curse, choose one ability score; the target has Disadvantage on ability checks made with that ability.
You can only have one creature cursed at a time, but this feature does not require concentration.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of Darkness
entries: You become a nightmare made manifest. You can cast {@spell greater invisibility} on yourself without providing any components. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Dexterity modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Master of the Abyss
entries: While you are Invisible or in an area of Heavy Obscurity, you can use two Cunning Strike options simultaneously on a single Sneak Attack, paying the Sneak Attack die cost for only the most expensive option.

subclassFeature.name: Marauder
entries: While some fighters rely on the elegance of a blade or the precision of an arrow, the Marauder relies on the brutal physics of the clinch. These warriors are specialists in close-quarters lockdown, trained to exploit every gap in an opponent's posture to drag them to the dirt or squeeze the life from their lungs. Often found as elite urban enforcers, pit fighters, or shock troopers, Marauders treat the battlefield like a wrestling mat where they are the undisputed masters.

subclassFeature.name: CQC Specialist
entries: Your training focuses on the brutal arts of the clinch and the takedown. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Unarmed Prowess
entries: Your unarmed strikes deal bludgeoning damage equal to {@damage 1d8} + your Strength modifier.

entries.name: Seamless Takedown
entries: When you take the Attack action and hit a creature with an Unarmed Strike, you can force them to make the saving throw against being Grappled or Shoved (your choice) as part of that same attack, rather than having to forfeit the damage.

entries.name: Heavyweight Grappler
entries: The saving throw DC to resist your Grapple or Shove attempts is calculated as 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus. Furthermore, you can grapple creatures up to two sizes larger than you.

subclassFeature.name: The Strangler's Hold
entries: When you have a creature Grappled, you can exert devastating control over their posture and breathing. You gain two maneuvers you can use against a creature currently grappled by you:

entries.name: Chokehold
entries: As a Bonus Action, you attempt to cut off the creature's air or blood flow. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, the creature is {@condition Restrained} until the grapple ends. While Restrained in this way, the creature is suffocating, cannot speak, and cannot cast spells requiring Verbal components.

entries.name: Gale-Force Trip
entries: When you hit a grappled creature with a melee attack, you can immediately force them to make a Strength saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, they are knocked {@condition Prone} and take additional bludgeoning damage equal to your Fighter level. Because a grappled creature has a Speed of 0, they cannot spend movement to stand up from Prone until they break the grapple.

subclassFeature.name: Mean Streak
entries: You have learned to ruthlessly capitalize on an opponent's compromised posture.

entries.name: Brutalized Advantage
entries: Whenever you roll damage against a creature that is {@condition Prone}, {@condition Grappled}, or {@condition Restrained}, you can roll the weapon or unarmed strike's damage dice twice and use the higher total.

entries.name: Meat Shield
entries: You know how to leverage your victim's body against incoming attacks. As long as you are grappling a creature of your size or larger, you gain Half-Cover (+2 to AC and Dexterity saving throws).

subclassFeature.name: Unstoppable Momentum
entries: Your physical conditioning reaches new heights, allowing you to dominate the arena without breaking a sweat.
Your Unarmed Prowess damage increases to {@damage 1d10}.
Your movement speed is no longer halved when you are dragging or carrying a grappled creature.

subclassFeature.name: Pursuer
entries: You become an inescapable force on the battlefield.

entries.name: Vaulting Leap
entries: Your jump distance is doubled. If you move at least 10 feet before leaping toward a creature, you can attempt to Grapple them as a Bonus Action upon landing.

entries.name: Submission Master
entries: Your Chokehold becomes lethal. When a creature starts its turn {@condition Restrained} by your Chokehold, they must succeed on a Constitution saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, they gain one level of Exhaustion. This exhaustion is removed when they finish a Short or Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Apex Predator
entries: You have reached the pinnacle of physical dominance. You gain the following:

entries.name: Colossal Grip
entries: You can now grapple creatures of any size.

entries.name: Neck Break
entries: When you have a creature {@condition Restrained} via your Chokehold, you can use your Action to attempt a horrific finishing move. The target must make a Constitution saving throw against your Grapple DC. On a failure, they take {@damage 10d10} Force damage, or half as much on a success. If this damage reduces the creature to 0 hit points, you snap their neck or spine, killing them instantly (provided the creature has an anatomy that allows for this).

subclassFeature.name: Fate Domain
entries: Gods of fate perceive the future not as a single thread, but as a sprawling tapestry of choices that drive mortals toward their destinies. Some deities consider the end preordained, while others understand the multiverse as a chaotic web of infinite possibility. Clerics who draw power from the forces of fate are weavers of this web. They receive fleeting omens, pluck the strings of probability to share impossible knowledge with their allies, and prophesize their enemies' inevitable doom.

entries.caption: Fate Domain Spells
rows: {@spell augury}, {@spell bless}, Fortune's Favor, {@spell silvery barbs}

subclassFeature.name: Prophet of Truth
entries: You can nudge the scales of destiny in moments of crisis. When you or a creature you can see within 30 feet makes a D20 Test (an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw), you can use your Reaction to roll a {@dice 1d6} and add or subtract it from the total. You can choose to use this feature after the d20 is rolled, but before the DM declares whether the roll succeeds or fails.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Strands of Fate
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to tether the fates of those around you. As a Magic Action, you choose a number of willing creatures you can see within 30 feet of you, up to your Wisdom modifier. For 1 minute, you create a glowing, ethereal Fate Bond between them and yourself. While bonded, you unlock the following capabilities:

entries.name: Tethered Vitality
entries: Whenever a bonded creature takes damage, you can use your Reaction to halve that damage. One other bonded creature of your choice (which can be you) then takes an amount of Force damage equal to the prevented damage. This transferred damage cannot be reduced or avoided in any way.

entries.name: Shared Destiny
entries: Once per round, when a bonded creature makes a D20 Test, they can choose to replace their roll with the natural d20 result most recently rolled by another bonded creature. They must choose to do this before the outcome of their roll is determined.

subclassFeature.name: Ties that Bind
entries: Your mastery over the timeline deepens, allowing you to sever harmful threads of fate. When you use your Strands of Fate Channel Divinity, you can immediately end one spell or condition affecting one of the bonded creatures. The condition must be {@condition Blinded}, {@condition Charmed}, {@condition Deafened}, {@condition Frightened}, {@condition Paralyzed}, or {@condition Poisoned}.

subclassFeature.name: Master of Destiny
entries: You have seen the end of all things, and nothing catches you off guard. You can no longer be Surprised, and you have Advantage on all Initiative rolls.

entries.name: Visions of the Future
entries: You can cast the {@spell foresight} spell once per Long Rest without expending a spell slot or requiring material components. When you cast it using this feature, its duration is reduced to 10 minutes, but you can choose one allied creature within 30 feet to also gain the full benefits of the spell for the duration.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Patron
entries: Your pact draws on the power of a dying god, a being once worshipped by countless followers but now reduced to an infant, an amnesiac, or a helpless avatar. This dying god, known as a vestige, might have been benevolent, evil, or strange and unknowable, but right now, it is entirely dependent on you. It needs your help to survive, learn about the world, and eventually regain its former power. In exchange, it shares its erratic, overwhelming strength with you—provided you can keep it in a good mood.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Companion
entries: The vestige manifests to aid you, drawing strength from your pact. It is a Celestial, Fiend, or Undead (choose when you gain this feature). You determine its appearance, but regardless of its form, it bears features indicating its supernatural origin. The vestige is Friendly to you and your allies and obeys your commands. It vanishes if you die.

entries.name: The Vestige in Combat
entries: In combat, the vestige acts during your turn. It can move and use its Reaction on its own, but the only action it takes is the Dodge action unless you take a Bonus Action to command it to take an action in its stat block or some other action. You can also sacrifice one of your attacks when you take the Attack action to command the vestige to take the Vestige's Strike action. If you have the {@condition Incapacitated} condition, the vestige acts on its own and isn't limited to the Dodge action.

entries.name: Divine Disposition
entries: The vestige's power is unstable, and its willingness to aid you depends on its current disposition. At the start of combat, its disposition is Neutral.

items: {@b Neutral:} The vestige follows your commands normally as outlined above.
{@b Displeased:} If the vestige takes damage, its disposition becomes Displeased. While Displeased, its Vestige's Strike attack rolls are made with Disadvantage.

{@b Favorable:} As a Bonus Action, you or an ally within 5 feet of the vestige can offer a prayer or tend to its vulnerable form by making a DC 10 Charisma (Persuasion or Religion) check. On a success, its disposition improves by one step. While Favorable, its Vestige's Strike attack rolls are made with Advantage.

entries.name: Disappearance of the Vestige
entries: When the vestige drops to 0 Hit Points, it disappears. You can spend 1 minute performing a magic ceremony to manifest the vestige again; it reappears with its maximum Hit Points in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. As a Magic action, you can temporarily dismiss the vestige to a pocket dimension, and as a Magic action while it is dismissed, you can cause it to reappear in an unoccupied space within 30 feet of you.
Whenever you finish a Long Rest, you can summon the vestige in a new form, choosing its appearance and whether it is a Celestial, Fiend, or Undead.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Spells
entries: The magic of your Vestige Companion ensures you always have certain spells ready. Select one of the following Cleric Domains: Life, Light, Trickery, or War. The Domain Spells of your chosen Domain are Warlock spells for you. When you reach a Warlock level equal to a Cleric Level listed on the Domain Spells table for the Domain you have chosen, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Power
entries: Once per day as a Bonus Action, your vestige manifests a remnant of its divine power in one of the following ways, based on its form. The vestige cannot use this feature while its disposition is Displeased.

entries.name: Cursed Invocation (Undead Only)
entries: The vestige places a curse on a creature you can see within 30 feet of the vestige for 1 minute. While cursed, the target has Disadvantage on attack rolls against you and the vestige.

entries.name: Fiendish Swap (Fiend Only)
entries: If you and the vestige are within 60 feet of each other, you both teleport, swapping places.

entries.name: Healing Touch (Celestial Only)
entries: The vestige touches another creature. The target regains Hit Points equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Charisma modifier and ends one condition on it: {@condition Blinded}, {@condition Deafened}, or {@condition Poisoned}.

subclassFeature.name: Vestige Recovery
entries: Your Vestige Companion now regains its use of Divine Power whenever you finish a Short or Long Rest or when you use your Magical Cunning feature. Additionally, if you spend time tending to the vestige during a Short Rest, its disposition automatically becomes Favorable at the start of your next combat encounter.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Power
entries: Your Vestige Companion increases in magical strength. As a Magic Action, you can cause the vestige to manifest an aura in a 30-foot Emanation that originates from the vestige. The aura lasts a number of hours equal to your Charisma modifier or until the vestige disappears or is temporarily dismissed. You, the vestige, and your allies within the aura gain Resistance to Fire, Necrotic, and Radiant damage and have Immunity to the {@condition Charmed} and {@condition Frightened} conditions. Once you manifest this aura, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

In addition, when you drop to 0 Hit Points while within the aura, you instead change your Hit Points to a number equal to your Warlock level plus your Charisma modifier. The vestige is then dismissed into a pocket dimension and can't return until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Semblance of Life
entries: Your Vestige Companion continues to grow in strength, and with your assistance, it can briefly adopt a more powerful form. Depending on the vestige's type, you can cast one of the following spells while the vestige is within 90 feet of you without expending a spell slot or needing Material components: {@spell summon celestial} (Celestial vestige), {@spell summon fiend} (Fiend vestige), or {@spell summon undead} (Undead vestige).

When you cast the spell with this feature, the vestige becomes the summoned creature. Its game statistics are replaced by the summoned creature's stat block, the spell's level is equal to half your Warlock level (round down; maximum level 9), and the spell's duration is 1 minute. When the spell ends, the vestige returns to its previous form. Once you have cast the spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Path of Wild Magic
entries: Many places in the multiverse abound with beauty, intense emotion, and rampant magic; the Feywild, the Upper Planes, and other realms of supernatural power radiate with such forces and can profoundly influence people. As folk of deep feeling, barbarians are especially susceptible to these wild influences, with some barbarians being transformed by the magic. These magic-suffused barbarians walk the Path of Wild Magic. Elf, tiefling, aasimar, and genasi barbarians often seek this path, eager to manifest the otherworldly magic of their ancestors.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Awareness
entries: Your connection to wild magic allows you to sense its presence. As a Magic Action, you can cast the {@spell detect magic} spell without expending a spell slot or requiring components. Strength is your spellcasting ability for this spell. When you cast it using this feature, its range is 60 feet, and it does not require Concentration.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Strength modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Wild Surge
entries: The magical energy roiling inside you sometimes erupts from you. When you enter your Rage, you roll on your Wild Magic d100 table to determine the magical effect produced.
If a Wild Magic effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Bolstering Magic
entries: You can harness your wild magic to bolster yourself or a companion. As a Magic Action, you can touch a willing creature (which can be yourself) and confer one of the following benefits of your choice:

entries.name: Might
entries: For 10 minutes, whenever the creature makes a D20 Test (an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw), it can roll a {@dice d4} and add it to the result.

entries.name: Mana
entries: The creature rolls a {@dice d4} and regains an expended spell slot of a level equal to the number rolled or lower (the creature's choice). Once a creature receives this benefit, it can't receive it again until it finishes a Long Rest.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Strength modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Precision
entries: The chaotic energy surrounding you parts the magical defenses of your foes. While your Rage is active, your melee weapon attacks ignore spells and magical effects that reduce, nullify, or reflect damage (such as {@spell armor of agathys}, {@spell fire shield}, or {@spell blink}). Standard damage Resistances and Immunities still apply normally.

subclassFeature.name: Unstable Backlash
entries: When you are imperiled, the magic within you lashes out defensively. When you take damage or fail a saving throw while your Rage is active, you can use your Reaction to roll on the Wild Magic d100 table and immediately produce the effect. This new effect replaces your current active Wild Magic effect.

subclassFeature.name: Controlled Surge
entries: You have learned to ride the chaotic currents of your magic. Whenever you roll on the Wild Magic d100 table, you can roll the die twice and choose which of the two effects to unleash. If you roll the exact same number on both dice, you can ignore the roll and choose any effect on the table.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Juggernaut
entries: Juggernaut barbarians are siege engines in their own right, able to stand their ground and move their foes into positions of weakness. While raging, they make any battlefield their domain, shoving foes over mountainsides and destroying even the strongest structures with their attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Thunderous Blows
entries: Starting when you choose this path, your rage instills you with the strength to batter around your foes, making any battlefield your domain. Once per turn while your Rage is active, when you damage a creature with a melee attack, you can force the target to make a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + your Strength modifier + your Proficiency Bonus).
On a failed save, you push the target 5 feet away from you, and you can choose to immediately move up to 5 feet into the target's previous space without provoking Opportunity Attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Stance of the Mountain
entries: You harness your fury to anchor your feet to the earth, shrugging off the blows of those who wish to topple you. While your Rage is active, you cannot be knocked {@condition Prone} unless you have the {@condition Unconscious} condition.

subclassFeature.name: Demolishing Might
entries: Beginning at 6th level, you muster destructive force with your assault, shaking the core of even the strongest structures. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Siege Engine
entries: Your melee attacks deal double damage to objects and structures. Furthermore, your melee attacks against creatures of the Construct type deal an additional {@damage 1d8} damage of the weapon's type.

entries.name: Diligent Discipline
entries: When you successfully push a creature or knock it {@condition Prone} (such as with an Unarmed Strike or a Weapon Mastery like Push or Topple), the next time you hit that creature with an improvised weapon or melee attack before the end of your turn, the attack deals an extra {@damage 1d8} Thunder damage, and the creature is pushed up to 15 feet away from you. This Thunder damage increases to {@damage 2d8} when you reach Barbarian level 10.

subclassFeature.name: Overwhelming Cleave
entries: Upon reaching 10th level, you wade into armies of foes, great swings of your weapon striking any who threaten you. While your Rage is active, when you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can use a Bonus Action to make one additional melee weapon attack against a different creature that is within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach.

subclassFeature.name: Unstoppable
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can choose to become an unstoppable force when you activate your Rage. If you do so, for the duration of the Rage your Speed cannot be reduced, and you are immune to the {@condition Frightened}, {@condition Paralyzed}, and {@condition Stunned} conditions.

If you are currently afflicted by the {@condition Frightened}, {@condition Paralyzed}, or {@condition Stunned} conditions, you can still take a Bonus Action to activate your Rage and suspend those effects for the duration of your Rage. When an Unstoppable Rage ends, the sheer physical toll causes you to gain one level of Exhaustion.

subclassFeature.name: The Seeker
entries: While some warlocks make pacts with fiends or fey, you have bound yourself to the pursuit of pure, unfiltered knowledge. You are a Seeker, a cosmic wanderer entrusted by an unfathomable patron to explore the furthest reaches of the Astral Plane and beyond. Your patron might be a deity of knowledge like Oghma or Celestian, a cosmic entity dwelling in the Far Realm, or an ancient mortal who transcended the bounds of reality.

entries.caption: Seeker Spells
rows: {@spell feather fall}, {@spell jump}, {@spell levitate}, {@spell locate object}

subclassFeature.name: Shielding Aurora
entries: You can invoke your patron's power to protect you from harm as you traverse dangerous realms. As a Bonus Action, you create a whirling aurora of brilliant cosmic energy that swirls around you.

Until the end of your next turn, you gain Resistance to all damage, and if a hostile creature ends its turn within 10 feet of you, it takes Radiant damage equal to your Warlock level + your Charisma modifier.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Chain of the Cosmos
entries: Your patron grants you a physical manifestation of your bond, often appearing as a chain forged from starlight, an ancient astrolabe, or a glowing pendant.

While this item is on your person, you always have the {@spell augury} spell prepared, and you can cast it as a Ritual without requiring Material components. Furthermore, you can invoke the item's power to gain Advantage on an Intelligence check. Once you use this Advantage feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest.
If you lose this item, you can perform a 1-hour ceremony during a Short or Long Rest to receive a replacement, which destroys the previous item.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Refuge
entries: You gain the ability to step into an astral refuge, dodging danger and rapidly preparing your magical defenses. As a Magic Action, you disappear from the world for a brief moment and enter the Astral Plane, taking advantage of its timeless nature.

While in your astral refuge, you can prepare to use two Actions to cast spells. After you prepare these two actions, you instantly return to the space you occupied (or the nearest unoccupied space), the spells are cast and your turn ends.
Once you use this feature, you cannot use it again until you finish a Short or Long Rest, unless you expend a Warlock spell slot (no action required) to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Far Wanderer
entries: Your body adapts to the harsh, unforgiving environments of the cosmos. You no longer need to breathe, and you gain Resistance to Fire damage and Cold damage.

subclassFeature.name: Astral Sequestration
entries: You gain the ability to sequester yourself and your allies within a localized pocket of the Astral Plane. By performing a special ritual over the course of 1 minute, you shift yourself and up to ten willing creatures you can see into the Astral Plane.

You and those creatures gain the benefits of a Short Rest while sequestered on the Astral Plane. You then return to the spaces you all occupied when you used this ability, with no time having passed in the material world. During this special Short Rest, you and the sequestered creatures can make use of any options normally available during a rest.
Once you use this ability, you cannot use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sea Sorcery
entries: Your innate magic comes from the vast and untamable power of the sea. You might be the descendant of a sea god, have been saved from drowning by a powerful elemental, or perhaps you were born in a storm so mighty it imprinted its magic upon your soul. Whatever its origin, your magic is the magic of the crashing wave, the dark pressure of the abyss, and the sudden, shocking strike of a storm. You are a conduit for the ocean's beautiful and terrifying power.

subclassFeature.name: Soul of the Sea
entries: Your body is imbued with the physical resilience of the deep. You can breathe air and water, and you gain a Swim Speed equal to your Speed.

subclassFeature.name: Aqueous Shield
entries: You can call upon the ocean to protect you. As a Bonus Action, you can spend 1 Sorcery Point to envelop yourself in a swirling shield of magical water for 1 minute. While the shield is active, you gain a +2 bonus to your Armor Class.

When a creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack, the shield violently erupts and the effect ends. The attacker takes Cold damage equal to your Charisma modifier plus your Proficiency Bonus, and the target becomes Drenched until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Drenched
entries: A drenched creature's Speed is reduced by 10 feet, and is considered wet.

subclassFeature.name: Tidal Escape
entries: You can momentarily dissolve into water to evade harm. When you are hit by an attack, you can use your Reaction and expend 1 Sorcery Point to gain Resistance to the triggering attack's damage. You then instantly teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 15 feet of you, reappearing in a splash of sea spray.

subclassFeature.name: Master of the Tides
entries: Your watery forms and shields become second nature. You gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Crashing Wave
entries: While your Aqueous Shield is active, you can use your Bonus Action to drench a foe from afar. Choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw against your Spell Save DC. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 2d8} Cold damage and becomes Drenched until the end of its next turn.

entries.name: Fluid Movement
entries: When you move on your turn, you take only half damage from Opportunity Attacks, and you can move through any enemy's space (though you cannot willingly end your move there).

entries.name: Water Form
entries: On your turn, you can move through any space that is at least 3 inches in diameter without squeezing. When you stop moving, the regular squeezing rules apply if you're in a space one size smaller than you. You can't willingly stop in a space smaller than that; if you're forced to do so, you immediately flow to the nearest space that can fit you, back along the path of your movement.

subclassFeature.name: Water Soul
entries: Your physical being is permanently altered by the power of the sea, transforming you into an elemental force. You gain the following benefits:

items: You no longer need to eat, drink, or sleep.
Any critical hit against you becomes a normal hit.
You have Resistance to Bludgeoning, Piercing, and Slashing damage.

subclassFeature.name: Order Domain
entries: The Order Domain represents discipline, as well as devotion to the laws that govern a society, an institution, or a philosophy. Clerics of Order meditate on logic and justice as they serve their gods.

Clerics of Order believe that well-crafted laws establish legitimate hierarchies, and those selected by law to lead must be obeyed. Those who obey must do so to the best of their ability, and if those who lead fail to protect the law, they must be replaced. In this manner, law weaves a web of obligations that create order and security in a chaotic multiverse.

entries.caption: Order Domain Spells
rows: {@spell command}, {@spell heroism}, {@spell hold person}, {@spell zone of truth}

subclassFeature.name: Voice of Authority
entries: You can invoke the power of law to embolden an ally to attack. If you cast a spell of level 1 or higher and target an allied creature with the spell, that ally can use their Reaction immediately after the spell's effects resolve to make one weapon attack or Unarmed Strike against a creature of your choice that you can see.
If the spell targets more than one ally, you choose the ally who gets to make the attack.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Order's Demand
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to exert an intimidating presence over others. As a Magic Action, you present your Holy Symbol, and each creature of your choice that can see or hear you within 30 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or gain the {@condition Charmed} condition until the end of your next turn, or until the Charmed creature takes any damage.
You can also cause any of the Charmed creatures to drop what they are holding when they fail the saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Embodiment of the Law
entries: You have become remarkably adept at channeling magical energy to compel others. If you cast a spell of the Enchantment school of level 1 or higher, you can change the spell's casting time to a Bonus Action for this casting, provided the spell's casting time is normally an Action.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses of it when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Grand Strategy
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to fuel your tactical mind, altering the flow of the battlefield. As a Magic Action, you choose one of the following options:

entries.name: Exploit Weakness
entries: You point out a tactical flaw in a creature or object you can see within 60 feet. For 1 minute, the first time you or an ally makes an attack roll or ability check against that target, the roll is made with Advantage. If it is an attack that hits, it deals an additional {@damage 2d8} damage of the weapon or spell's type. Once the Advantage is used, this effect ends.

entries.name: Plant Weakness
entries: You seed doubt and sabotage within an enemy's defenses. Choose one creature you can see within 60 feet. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your Spell Save DC. On a failure, you strip away its tactical advantages: for 1 minute, the target cannot benefit from Half-Cover or Three-Quarters Cover, and it subtracts a {@dice 1d4} from the next saving throw it makes.

subclassFeature.name: Order's Wrath
entries: Enemies you designate for destruction wilt under the combined efforts of you and your allies. When you use your Voice of Authority feature to allow an ally to make an attack, you empower that attack with the heavy, crushing weight of the law. If that specific attack hits, it deals an extra {@damage 2d10} Psychic damage.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Knight
entries: A Grave Knight is a warrior who has tethered their soul to the grim energies of the necropolis. They are not necessarily evil, but they understand that death is a tool, a source of power, and an inevitable part of the cycle. Drawing power from this connection, a Grave Knight wreathes their blade in necrotic energy, teleports through shadowy paths, and channels the chilling touch of the beyond.

subclassFeature.name: Gravecasting
entries: You have learned to draw upon the magic of the grave.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn two cantrips of your choice from the Warlock spell list.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Grave Knight Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells of 1st level and higher. To cast one of these spells, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Spells Known
entries: You know three 1st-level Warlock spells of your choice, which must be from the Abjuration, Conjuration, or Necromancy schools. Whenever you gain a Fighter level, you can replace one of your known spells with another from those schools.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for your Warlock spells, as your power comes from esoteric study of death's rites. Your Spell Save DC is 8 + your Intelligence modifier + your Proficiency Bonus.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Rites
entries: You have learned martial rites that let you call on death's small favors, fueled by a special pool of energy. You have a number of Grave Rites equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You regain all expended Grave Rites when you finish a Short or Long Rest.
You can expend these Rites to fuel the following features:

entries.name: Dread Smite
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack or Unarmed Strike, you can expend 1 Grave Rite to channel the {@spell inflict wounds} spell directly through the blow. The attack deals an extra {@damage 3d10} Necrotic damage. If you have spell slots of 2nd-level or higher, this extra damage increases by 1d10 for each slot level above 1st (e.g., {@damage 4d10} if your highest slot is 2nd-level).

entries.name: Grave Step
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can expend 1 Grave Rite to wrap yourself in the energies of the grave and teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.

subclassFeature.name: Rigor Mortis
entries: Your connection to the grave hardens your physical form against harm. Whenever you roll Initiative or use your Second Wind feature, you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to your Fighter level + your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Rebuke
entries: When a creature you can see damages you, you can use your Reaction and expend 1 Grave Rite to point your finger and surround them in ghostly flames. The creature must make a Dexterity saving throw against your Spell Save DC. It takes {@damage 2d10} Necrotic damage and {@damage 2d10} Fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

subclassFeature.name: Soul Siphon
entries: Your command over necrotic energies allows you to draw vitality from your foes. When you deal Necrotic damage to a creature with a Grave Knight feature or a spell, you can siphon their life force, regaining Hit Points equal to half the Necrotic damage dealt.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of Undeath
entries: As a Magic Action, you can become a conduit for the grave's power for 1 minute. For the duration, you gain the following benefits:

items: You have Resistance to Necrotic and Poison damage.
You have Advantage on saving throws against being {@condition Frightened} or {@condition Paralyzed}.
Your melee weapon attacks deal an additional {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage.
When you use your Grave Step feature, you can make one melee weapon attack against a target within your reach as part of that same Bonus Action.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest.

subclassFeature.name: Entropy Domain
entries: While most gods focus on creation or preservation, deities of Entropy represent the fundamental truth that all things must end. This domain includes Ao, the Overgod who stands above the pantheon, overseeing the cosmic balance and the eventual dissolution of cycles. To an Entropy cleric, chaos is not a lack of order, but the natural state to which the universe yearns to return. They are the heralds of the heat death of the universe, the rust on the blade, and the cold silence between stars. They do not necessarily seek to destroy; they simply ensure that what is meant to pass away does not linger.

entries.caption: Entropy Domain Spells
rows: {@spell armor of agathys}, {@spell bane}, Magnify Gravity, {@spell ray of enfeeblement}

subclassFeature.name: Touch of the Void
entries: You can accelerate the decay of magic and matter. When a creature you can see within 30 feet makes a D20 Test, you can use your Reaction to impose Disadvantage on that roll as you sap the energy from their effort.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest. Additionally, whenever you deal damage with a Cleric cantrip, you can change the damage type to Necrotic or Force.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Entropic Collapse
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to create a localized zone of absolute disorder. As a Magic Action, you present your holy symbol and designate a 15-foot cube centered on a point within 60 feet. The area becomes Difficult Terrain for 1 minute. While the zone persists, you gain the following benefits:

entries.name: Erosion of Will
entries: Any creature that starts its turn in the zone or enters it for the first time on a turn must succeed on a Constitution Saving Throw against your Spell Save DC or take {@damage 2d6} Force damage and have its Speed halved until the start of its next turn.

entries.name: Energy Absorption
entries: If a creature within the zone deals damage with a spell, you can use your Reaction to reduce that damage by an amount equal to your Cleric Level + Wisdom Modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Forecasting
entries: The entropic energies you wield begin to feed back into your own spells. You can add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any Cleric spell.

Furthermore, your Entropic Collapse now severs the ties of magical effects. When a creature fails its saving throw against your Entropic Collapse, one spell of 3rd level or lower currently affecting that creature ends immediately.

subclassFeature.name: Herald of the End
entries: You have become an avatar of the ultimate vacuum. You gain Resistance to Necrotic and Force damage, and you are immune to the Exhaustion condition.

entries.name: Singularity of Ao
entries: You can cast the {@spell antimagic field} spell once per Long Rest without expending a spell slot. When you cast it using this feature, you can designate a number of creatures up to your Wisdom modifier; the field does not suppress their magical items or spells they cast.
Additionally, while this field is active, any creature that dies within it is reduced to fine gray dust, and can only be restored to life by a {@spell true resurrection} or {@spell wish} spell.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of the Spellguard
entries: Paladins who swear the Oath of the Spellguard are the militant arm of the Church of Mystra. Known amongst the clergy as the Spellblades, these elite inquisitors are tasked with hunting down heretics, shadow magic practitioners, and those who abuse the arcane arts to subvert the natural order of magic. They serve as unbreakable shields for Mystra's chosen mages, utilizing divine barriers and ancient counter-magic techniques to turn a heretic's power against them.

entries.name: Tenets of the Spellguard
entries: A paladin who takes this oath embodies the unyielding defense of the Weave, swearing to protect its faithful and punish its defilers.

items: {@b Preserve the Weave.} Magic is Mystra's sacred gift. You must strike down those who twist it into abominations or use it to subjugate the innocent.
{@b Shield the Faithful.} You are the bulwark. Stand between Mystra's servants and harm, absorbing the blows meant for the physically frail.
{@b Punish the Heretic.} Let every strike against the natural order of magic be met with divine retribution. Ensure that those who practice forbidden arts face the ultimate justice.

entries.caption: Oath of the Spellguard Spells
rows: {@spell Circle of Power}, {@spell Mass Cure Wounds}

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Mystran Proclamation
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to draw the ire of your enemies and become a living shield. As a Bonus Action, you utter a divine challenge. For 1 minute, any hostile creature within 30 feet of you has Disadvantage on attack rolls against targets other than you. Additionally, while this proclamation is active, if an ally within 5 feet of you is targeted by an attack, you can magically transpose yourself to become the target of that attack instead (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Weave-Bound Aegis
entries: You can use your Channel Divinity to deploy a perfect barrier. When you or a creature within 30 feet of you takes damage from an attack or spell, you can use your Reaction to grant them Invulnerability to one damage type of your choice from that attack (e.g., slashing, fire, necrotic). This Invulnerability lasts only for the duration of that single attack or spell, reducing the damage of the chosen type to 0.

entries.name: Staggering Impact
entries: An enemy whose attack strikes a target with Invulnerability has their momentum shattered; the attacker is staggered and immediately forfeits any remaining extra attacks or Actions they possess for the current turn.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Retribution
entries: Your defensive presence actively punishes heretics who strike your frontline. You and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you gain Resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage.

Additionally, the kinetic force of any blow against you is violently repulsed. Whenever an enemy hits you or an ally within your aura with a melee attack, the attacker takes Radiant damage equal to your Charisma modifier.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Rebuke the Heretic
entries: Your mastery over Mystra's barriers allows you to perfectly rebound magical and physical assaults. As a Reaction when an ally within 30 feet of you takes damage from an attack or spell, you can immediately reduce the incoming damage to 0. The attacking creature then takes Radiant damage equal to the amount of damage their attack or spell would have originally dealt.

Additionally, the purity of your magic cleanses the wounded. Whenever you use a spell or feature to restore hit points to a creature, you can automatically end one of the following conditions afflicting them: Blinded, Charmed, Deafened, Paralyzed, or Poisoned.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of the Magic
entries: At 20th level, you can embody the absolute defense of Mystra herself. As a Bonus Action, you gain the following benefits for 1 minute:

items: {@b Absolute Repel:} You gain Invulnerability to all bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage. Any such damage directed at you is completely nullified, and enemies who strike you forfeit their remaining Actions for the current turn.
{@b Weave Guard:} You and allies within your Aura of Retribution gain Resistance to all damage dealt by spells and magical effects.
{@b Divine Revitalization:} The Weave continuously mends your wounds. You regain 10 hit points and recover one expended 1st-level spell slot at the start of each of your turns.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a Long Rest. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending a 5th-level spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Warrior of the Mystic Arts
entries: Warriors of the Mystic Arts wield magic to supplement their martial skill. They harness their supernatural focus to enhance their magical and physical abilities.

subclassFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: You have learned to cast spells. See the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB|7} for the rules on spellcasting. The information below details how you use those rules as a Warrior of the Mystic Arts.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You know two cantrips of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list. {@spell Blade Ward} and {@spell Thunderclap|XPHB} are recommended. Whenever you gain a Monk level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another cantrip of your choice from the Sorcerer spell list.
When you reach Monk level 10, you learn another Sorcerer cantrip of your choice.

entries.name: Spell Slots
entries: The Warrior of the Mystic Arts Spellcasting table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your level 1+ spells. You regain all expended slots when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Prepared Spells of Level 1+
entries: You prepare the list of level 1+ spells that are available for you to cast with this feature. To start, choose three level 1 spells from the Sorcerer spell list. {@spell Jump}, {@spell Magic Missile}, and {@spell Shield} are recommended.

The number of spells on your list increases as you gain Monk levels, as shown in the Prepared Spells column of the Warrior of the Mystic Arts Spellcasting table. Whenever that number increases, choose additional spells from the Sorcerer spell list until the number of spells on your list matches the number on the table. The chosen spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots. For example, if you're a level 7 Monk, your list of prepared spells can include five Sorcerer spells of levels 1 and 2 in any combination.

entries.name: Changing Your Prepared Spells
entries: Whenever you gain a Monk level, you can replace one spell on your list with another Sorcerer spell for which you have spell slots.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for your Sorcerer spells.

entries.name: Spellcasting Focus
entries: You can use an {@item Arcane Focus|XPHB} as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Sorcerer spells.

entries.name: Multiclassing
entries: If you multiclass and have the Spellcasting feature from more than one class, add one third of your Monk levels (round down) to determine your available spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Mystic Fighting Style
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can replace one of the attacks with a casting of {@filter one of your Sorcerer cantrips that has a casting time of an action|spells|level=0|class=sorcerer|cast time=action}.

subclassFeature.name: Mystic Focus
entries: You keep your magical power and martial focus in perfect balance, allowing you to convert spell slots into Focus Points, or convert Focus Points into spell slots.

entries.name: Converting Spell Slots to Focus Points
entries: You can expend a spell slot to regain a number of expended Focus Points equal to the slot's level (no action required).

entries.name: Recovering Spell Slots
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can transform unexpended Focus Points to recover one expended spell slot. The {@table Recovering Spell Slots|XUA2026MysticSubclasses} table shows the cost of recovering a spell slot of a given level, and it lists the minimum Monk level you must be to recover a slot. You can recover a spell slot no higher than level 4.

subclassFeature.name: Centered Focus
entries: Whenever you expend a Focus Point to use {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, {@classFeature Patient Defense|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, or {@classFeature Step of the Wind|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any saving throw you make to maintain {@status Concentration} until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Mystic Fighting Style
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, you can replace two of the attacks with a casting of {@filter one of your level 1 or 2 Sorcerer spells that has a casting time of an action|spells|level=1;2|class=sorcerer|cast time=action}.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Stealer
entries: While ordinary thieves are cutting purses and burgling homes, a Magic Stealer is after a much more valuable prize: magic. The Magic Stealer preys on spellcasters, repurposing their magic and eluding divination spells to take magical power at the point of a knife.

subclassFeature.name: Drain Magic
entries: You can drain power from ongoing spells to recharge your allies' magic. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} Action, you can touch a willing creature and end one ongoing level 1 or level 2 spell on it; the creature immediately recovers one expended spell slot of level 2 or lower (the target's choice).
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Empower Sneak Attack
entries: Immediately after a creature you can see within 30 feet of you casts a level 1+ spell, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to absorb magical energy from the spell. When you do so, until the end of your next turn, the next time you hit with your {@classFeature Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|1|XPHB} you deal extra Force damage. To determine the extra damage, roll a number of {@dice d6|d6s} equal to the spell's level, and add them together.

You can take this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Magical Sabotage
entries: You gain the following Cunning Strike options.

subclassFeature.name: Disrupt Spell (Cost: 3d6)
entries: The target's magical acuity is disrupted until the start of your next turn. Whenever the target casts a spell during that time, it must succeed on an Intelligence saving throw or the spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.

subclassFeature.name: Spell Susceptibility (Cost: 2d6)
entries: The target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes against a spell until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Steal Resistance (Cost: 2d6)
entries: Choose one kind of damage. If the target has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that kind of damage, until the start of your next turn, the target loses that {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} and you gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to that damage type.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Drain Magic
entries: You can now use {@subclassFeature Drain Magic|Rogue|XPHB|MAgic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses|3|XUA2026MysticSubclasses} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. In addition, when you use {@subclassFeature Drain Magic|Rogue|XPHB|MAgic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses|3|XUA2026MysticSubclasses}, you can end one ongoing level 1, level 2, or level 3 spell on the target, and the target recovers one expended spell slot of level 3 or lower (the target's choice).

subclassFeature.name: Occult Shroud
entries: Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can cast the {@spell Nondetection} spell, using Intelligence as your spellcasting ability. When you do so, you can target only yourself, and the duration increases to 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Eldritch Implosion
entries: When you use {@subclassFeature Empower Sneak Attack|Rogue|XPHB|MAgic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses|3|XUA2026MysticSubclasses}, you can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw ({@dc 8} plus your Dexterity modifier and {@table Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failed save, the spell dissipates with no effect, and the target has the {@condition Stunned} condition until the start of its next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Archer
entries: An Arcane Archer studies a unique elven method of archery that weaves magic into attacks to produce supernatural effects. The first Arcane Archers stood watch over their communities and used magic-infused arrows to defeat monsters and invaders. Over the centuries, other species have learned this method of blending magical aptitude with archery. These archers have broadened this method's application to ranged weapons of many kinds and to aspects of adventuring life outside of combat.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Shot
entries: You learn to unleash special magical effects with your shots.

entries.name: Arcane Shot Options
entries: You learn two Arcane Shot options of your choice from the "{@subclassFeature Arcane Shot Options|Fighter|XPHB|Arcane Archer|ASRDR|3|ASRDR}" section later in this subclass's description.

You learn an additional Arcane Shot option of your choice when you reach Fighter levels 7, 10, 15, and 18. Each time you learn a new Arcane Shot option, you can also replace one option you know with a different one.

entries.name: Using Arcane Shot
entries: Once per turn when you make a ranged attack using a weapon with the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} property, you can apply one of your Arcane Shot options to that attack. You decide to use the option when you hit a creature and deal damage to it unless the option doesn't involve an attack roll.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Arcane Shot Die
entries: Arcane Shot options refer to your Arcane Shot Die. Your Arcane Shot Die is a {@dice d6}.

entries.name: Saving Throws
entries: If an Arcane Shot option requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Intelligence modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}.

entries.name: At Higher Levels
entries: Your Arcane Shot Die changes when you reach certain {@class Fighter|XPHB} levels. The die becomes a {@dice d8} at level 10, a {@dice d10} at level 15, and a {@dice d12} at level 18.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Shot Options
entries: The Arcane Shot options are presented here in alphabetical order.

subclassFeature.name: Curving Shot
entries: You learn how to direct an errant shot toward a new target. If you make an attack roll with a weapon with the {@itemProperty A|XPHB|Ammunition} property and miss, you can cause the shot to ricochet toward a new target as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} immediately after the attack misses. The new target must be a creature you can see within the weapon's range and within 60 feet of the attack's original target. Make an attack roll against the new target.

subclassFeature.name: Magical Ammunition
entries: You learn to imbue your ammunition with magical properties. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can imbue a piece of nonmagical ammunition with one of the following magical properties and fire it at a solid surface you can see within the weapon's range. On a hit, the ammunition's effect activates, and the ammunition attaches to the surface it hit for the duration of the effect; when the effect ends, the ammunition is destroyed.

Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of this feature by expending a use of your {@classFeature Second Wind|Fighter|XPHB|1|XPHB} (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Darkening Shot
entries: Magical shadows fill a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the ammunition for 1 minute. Nonmagical flames in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} are extinguished, and creatures in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} have a −5 penalty to Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks and {@variantrule Passive Perception|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Unlocking Shot
entries: A burst of magic fills a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the ammunition. A loud knock, audible up to 300 feet away, also emits from the ammunition. Any object in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} that is held shut by a mundane lock or that is stuck or barred becomes unlocked, unstuck, or unbarred. If the object has multiple locks, only one of them is unlocked.

subclassFeature.name: Vine Shot
entries: A 60-foot-long vine grows from the ammunition. You and other creatures can then climb it. The vine withers away after 10 minutes.

subclassFeature.name: Ever-Ready Shot
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB}, you can regain one expended use of Arcane Shot.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Burst
entries: You can push creatures away from you with your arcane mastery. When you use {@classFeature Indomitable|Fighter|XPHB|9|XPHB}, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you must succeed on a Strength saving throw against your Arcane Shot DC or be pushed up to 20 feet straight away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Masterful Shots
entries: You employ agility in your sharpshooting When a creature you can see misses you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to move up to half your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} away from the attacker without provoking {@action Opportunity Attack|XPHB|Opportunity Attacks}. You can then make a ranged attack roll against the attacker as part of this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} if the attacker is within the weapon's range.

subclassFeature.name: Tattooed Warrior
entries: Drawing from body-marking traditions from across the multiverse, Tattooed Warriors can use arcane power imbued in their magic tattoos. These Monks gain tattoos as they grow in martial prowess and insight. Tattooed Warriors can reshape their tattoos to access a suite of physical and magical effects.

subclassFeature.name: Beast Tattoos
entries: You gain two animal tattoos. Choose two tattoos from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Magic Tattoos
entries: You gain the magic tattoos described by other features of this subclass. The tattoos appear on your body wherever you wish. Damage or injury doesn't impair your magic tattoos' function. A magic tattoo can look like a brand, scarification, a birthmark, patterns of scales, or any other cosmetic alteration.

If a tattoo's effect requires a saving throw, the DC equals 8 plus your Wisdom modifier plus your {@variantrule Proficiency|XPHB|Proficiency Bonus}. Your spellcasting ability for spells granted by a tattoo is Wisdom.
Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can reshape one of your magic tattoos, changing the option you chose from one list to another option on the same list.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Tattoo
entries: You gain an additional magic tattoo depicting a celestial phenomenon. Choose a tattoo from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Nature Tattoo
entries: You gain an additional magic tattoo depicting a natural feature. Choose a tattoo from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Monster Tattoo
entries: You gain a magic tattoo depicting a mighty creature. Choose a tattoo from the following options.

subclassFeature.name: Conjurer
entries: You consider distance and matter to be flexible guidelines rather than physical laws. Conjurers harness the power of magic that moves creatures instantly through space and summons creatures to fight on their behalf.

subclassFeature.name: Benign Transposition
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see. Alternatively, you can choose a space within range that is occupied by a Medium or smaller creature. If that creature is willing, you both teleport, swapping places.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Distant Transposition
entries: The range of your {@subclassFeature Benign Transposition|Wizard|XPHB|Conjurer|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} feature increases to 60 feet. Additionally, you now regain all expended uses of it when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Durable Summons
entries: When you cast a Conjuration spell to summon or create a creature using a spell slot, that creature gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to twice your Wizard level when it first appears. While it has these {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB}, the creature has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to every damage type except Force, Necrotic, Psychic, and Radiant.

subclassFeature.name: Splintered Summons
entries: When you cast {@spell Summon Aberration|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Construct|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Dragon|XPHB}, {@spell Summon Elemental|XPHB}, or {@spell Summon Fey|XPHB} using a spell slot, you can modify the spell to summon two creatures with the spell instead of one. Each creature is of the same kind, uses the stat block and rules denoted by the spell, and manifests in a different unoccupied space of your choice within the spell's range, but the summoned creatures' {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are halved. If you lose {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell, both creatures disappear.

Once you use this feature to modify a spell in this way, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you do so again. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 5+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Enchanter
entries: Your magic clouds or captivates minds. Some Enchanters use their abilities to encourage peace and soften cruelty, while others use their mind-altering magic for selfish ends. Many Enchanters fall somewhere in between.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanting Conversationalist
entries: You gain proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice:  {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, or {@skill Persuasion}.
In addition, when you make an ability check with the chosen skill, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).

subclassFeature.name: Hypnotic Presence
entries: Your charming words and enchanting gaze can enthrall another creature. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, choose one creature that you can see within 10 feet of yourself. If the target can see or hear you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for 1 minute or until your {@status Concentration|XPHB} ends, the target is more than 10 feet away from you, the target can neither see nor hear you, or the target takes damage. While {@condition Charmed|XPHB} in this way, the target has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0.
Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 1+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Split Enchantment
entries: When you cast an Enchantment spell, such as {@spell Charm Person|XPHB}, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, increase the spell's effective level by 1.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier and regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Instinctive Charm
entries: When a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attack misses instead, and if there is another creature within range of the attack other than the attacker, the attacker targets that creature with the triggering attack, using the same attack roll. If multiple creatures are within the attack's range, you choose which one to target.

Once you use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by casting an Enchantment spell with a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Alter Memories
entries: You can reliably enchant creatures and alter their memories. You always have the {@spell Modify Memory|XPHB} spell prepared. When you cast the spell, you can target a second creature with it if that creature is within range of the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancer
entries: You explore the cosmic forces of life, death, and undeath. As a Necromancer, you learn to manipulate the energy that animates all creatures. As you progress, you learn to use magic to drain the life force from a creature and transform its vital energy into magical power. Many see Necromancers as menacing or villainous, though not all Necromancers are evil. Nevertheless, the manipulation of life and death is considered taboo in many societies.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancy Spellbook
entries: The necromantic secrets in your spellbook grant you additional powers. You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Necrotic Resistance
entries: You have {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

entries.name: Grim Harvest
entries: When you cast a Necromancy spell using a spell slot, you can choose an Undead creature you can see within 60 feet of yourself to regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the level of the spell slot expended plus your Wizard level.

entries.name: Undead Familiar
entries: The {@spell Find Familiar|XPHB} spell appears in your spellbook. When you cast the spell, you choose one of the normal forms for your familiar or one of the following special forms: {@creature Skeleton|XMM} or {@creature Zombie|XMM} (see {@book appendix B|XPHB|9} of the {@book Player's Handbook|XPHB} for the familiar's stat block).

subclassFeature.name: Undead Thralls
entries: You always have {@spell Animate Dead|XPHB} prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. Whenever you start casting the spell, you can modify it so that the spell's effective level is increased by 1.
In addition, whenever you cast a Necromancy spell using a spell slot that creates or summons an Undead, the Undead gains the benefits below.

entries.name: Undead Fortitude
entries: The Undead's Hit Point maximum and current {@variantrule Hit points|XPHB} increase by a number equal to the level of the spell slot used plus your Intelligence modifier for the duration of the spell.

entries.name: Withering Strike
entries: Whenever the Undead hits a creature with an attack roll, the Undead deals extra Necrotic damage equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 1 Necrotic damage).

subclassFeature.name: Harvest Undead
entries: You have learned more secrets about the nuances of life and death. Immediately after you become {@status Bloodied|XPHB} but aren't killed outright from taking damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce an Undead creature under your control that you can see to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. You then immediately regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wizard level.

subclassFeature.name: Death's Master
entries: Abstruse rituals within your spellbook allow you mastery over forces of death. While holding your spellbook, you gain the following benefits.

subclassFeature.name: Extinguish Undead
entries: When an Undead creature you can see is reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you can cause it to explode with necrotic energy. Roll a number of {@dice d6|d6s} equal to half the creature's unexpended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} (round up, minimum of {@dice 1d6}) and add them together. Each creature in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the Undead makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes Necrotic damage equal to the number rolled and can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions} until the start of its next turn. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only. When you use this feature to explode an Undead creature you don't control, you must take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and expend a level 5+ spell slot to do so.

subclassFeature.name: Transmuter
entries: You study spells that modify energy and matter. To you, the world is not a fixed thing, but eminently mutable. Your magic gives you the tools to become a smith at reality's forge.
Some Transmuters are tinkerers and pranksters. Others pursue their magical studies with deadly seriousness, seeking the power make and destroy worlds.

subclassFeature.name: Transmuter's Stone
entries: When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create a magic stone that lasts until you use this feature again. The stone is a Tiny object, and you can use it as a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB} for your Wizard spells. A creature with the stone in its possession gains proficiency in Constitution saving throws and one of the following benefits, which you choose when you create the stone. You can change the stone's benefit when you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: The bearer gains {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 60 feet or increases the range of its {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} by 60 feet.

entries.name: Speed
entries: The bearer's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Resistance
entries: The bearer gains Resistance to Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, Poison, or Thunder damage (your choice each time you choose this benefit).

subclassFeature.name: Wondrous Enhancement
entries: You always have the {@spell Alter Self|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.
While under the effects of {@spell Alter Self|XPHB}, you gain an additional benefit for each of its options.

entries.name: Aquatic Adaptation
entries: While underwater, you can take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Change Appearance
entries: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Charisma ({@skill Deception|XPHB}) checks.

entries.name: Natural Weapons
entries: The damage of your new growth increases to {@damage 2d6} damage of the type associated with the growth. You also have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Empowered Transmutation
entries: When you cast a Transmutation spell that doesn't deal damage, such as {@spell Fly|XPHB} or {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB|Magical Weapon}, using a spell slot, you can treat the spell as if it were cast using a spell slot 1 level above the slot expended.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Stone
entries: Your Transmuter's Stone is more versatile. When you create your Transmuter's Stone, you can choose up to two benefits. You can choose each option other than Resistance only once. If you choose Resistance twice, you must choose different damage types. You can change either or both benefits when you cast a Transmutation spell using a spell slot.
In addition, the following are now among your benefit options for Transmuter's Stone.

entries.name: Mighty Build
entries: The bearer has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength saving throws. The bearer also counts as one size larger when determining its carrying capacity.

entries.name: Tremorsense
entries: The bearer gains {@sense Tremorsense|XPHB} with a range of 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Shapechanger
entries: You always have the {@spell Polymorph|XPHB} spell prepared and can cast it once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast it in this way when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

In addition, when you target yourself with the spell, you can modify the spell to gain the benefits below. Once you modify the spell using this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

entries.name: Game Statistics
entries: You retain your personality, memories, and ability to speak. You also retain your Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores; class features; languages; and feats.

entries.name: Transmute Spells
entries: You can cast Transmutation spells while shape-shifted, except for any spell with a Material component that has a cost specified or is consumed by the spell.

subclassFeature.name: Master Transmuter
entries: While you carry your Transmuter's Stone, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to consume the reserve of transmutation magic stored inside and choose one of the following benefits. After you use your Transmuter's Stone in this way, the stone crumbles to dust. You can prevent the stone from crumbling by expending a level 5+ spell slot as part of the {@action Magic|XPHB} action you take using this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Major Transformation
entries: You can transmute one nonmagical object—no larger than a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} or eight connected 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cubes}—into another nonmagical object of similar size and mass and of equal or lesser value. You must spend 10 minutes handling the object to transform it.

subclassFeature.name: Panacea
entries: You touch a creature as part of this {@action Magic|XPHB} action, and the target regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half its Hit Point maximum (round down). The target is cured of all magical contagions, and any curses affecting the target are lifted, including the target's {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to a cursed item. If the target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} or {@condition Petrified|XPHB} conditions, those conditions end.

subclassFeature.name: Restore Life
entries: You cast the {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB} spell without expending a spell slot, using the stone in place of the required Material components.

subclassFeature.name: Restore Youth
entries: You touch one willing creature as part of this {@action Magic|XPHB} action, and the target's {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases to 0 and it permanently appears {@dice 3d10} years younger, to a minimum of young adulthood.

subclassFeature.name: Arcana Domain
entries: Magic suffuses the multiverse and fuels both destruction and creation. Clerics with this domain view magical knowledge not as power to be used in pursuit of personal ends, but as a gift they have the responsibility to share.

Gods associated with the Arcana Domain know the secrets and potential of magic intimately. These gods are often connected to knowledge, as learning and arcane power tend to go hand-in-hand, or to secrecy or power.

subclassFeature.name: Arcana Domain Spells
entries: Your connection to this divine domain means you always have certain spells ready. When you reach a Cleric level specified in the {@table Arcana Domain Spells|ASRDR} table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Initiate
entries: You gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Arcane Knowledge
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill if you don't have it already. You also gain {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill.

entries.name: Cantrips
entries: You learn {@filter two Wizard cantrips|spells|level=0|class=wizard} of your choice. Whenever you gain a Cleric level, you can replace one of these cantrips with another Wizard cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Modify Magic
entries: You can use your {@classFeature Channel Divinity|Cleric|XPHB|2|XPHB} to alter your spells as you cast them. When you cast a spell, you can expend one use of your {@classFeature Channel Divinity|Cleric|XPHB|2|XPHB} and change the spell in one of the following ways (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Fortifying Spell
entries: One target of the spell gains a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your Cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: Tenacious Spell
