entries: When you cast a spell that forces a creature to make a saving throw, choose one target of the spell you can see. Roll {@dice 1d6} and apply the number rolled as a penalty to the target's saving throw.

subclassFeature.name: Dispelling Recovery
entries: Immediately after you cast a spell with a spell slot that restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} to a creature or ends a condition on a creature, you can cast {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} on the creature as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} without expending a spell slot.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Mastery
entries: You learn four Wizard spells, one from each of levels {@filter 6|spells|level=6|class=wizard}, {@filter 7|spells|level=7|class=wizard}, {@filter 8|spells|level=8|class=wizard}, and {@filter 9|spells|level=9|class=wizard}. You thereafter always have those spells prepared. Whenever you gain a Cleric level, you can replace one of these spells with another Wizard spell of the same level.

subclassFeature.name: Arcane Archer Lore
entries: You learn magical theory and secrets of nature, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Cantrip
entries: You know either the {@spell Druidcraft|XPHB} or {@spell Prestidigitation|XPHB} cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Skills
entries: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} and {@skill Nature|XPHB} skills. If you already have one of these proficiencies, you instead gain proficiency in a different skill of your choice (or with two other skills if you have both).

subclassFeature.name: Ever Ready Shot
entries: When you roll {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} and have no uses of Arcane Shot left, you regain one expended use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Shots
entries: Your Arcane Shot Die becomes a {@dice d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Powerful Shots
entries: Your Arcane Shot Die becomes a {@dice d10}.

subclassFeature.name: Ancestral Sorcery
entries: Your innate magic comes from a specific ancestor who wielded such awesome magical power that a fragment of their personality guides you. This ancestor grants you guidance and direction as you explore your innate magical abilities. You might be the ancestor's sole surviving descendant, a reincarnation who bears an eerie resemblance to your ancestor, or a victim of a curse gained from handling your ancestor's personal effects.

subclassFeature.name: Ancestor's Lore
entries: You use your force of personality to channel your ancestor's knowledge. When you make an Intelligence check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of +1).
You also gain proficiency in one of these skills of your choice: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill History}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Nature}, or {@skill Religion}.

subclassFeature.name: Ancestral Spells
entries: When you reach the Sorcerer level specified in the {@table Lineage Spells|ASRDR|Ancestral Spells} table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Visage of the Ancestor
entries: Choose the form your ancestor takes, which might resemble the ancestor in life or a symbolic creature. While your {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1|XPHB} feature is active, this form appears in a spectral haze around you, and you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on any ability check you make as part of the {@action Influence|XPHB} action.

subclassFeature.name: Superior Spell Disruption
entries: Your ancestor's spellcasting mastery aids you in breaking spells. You always have {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} and {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} prepared.

While your {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1|XPHB} feature is active, you can cast each spell without expending a spell slot. If you cast {@spell Counterspell|XPHB} in this way, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its Constitution saving throw. If you cast {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} in this way, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your ability checks to end ongoing spells. Once you cast either spell without a spell slot, you must finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} before you can cast the spell in this way again.

subclassFeature.name: Ancestral Majesty
entries: Your ancestor's visage evokes awe or dread. While your {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1|XPHB} feature is active, you are surrounded by a magical aura in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Whenever a creature you can see enters the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition or has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn (your choice). A creature makes this save only once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: Steady Spellcaster
entries: Your deep connection with your ancestor steadies you. Taking damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on Sorcerer spells.

subclassFeature.name: Ancestor's Ward
entries: Your ancestor's protection redirects harmful magic away from you. While your {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1|XPHB} feature is active, you gain {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells. Once during your use of {@classFeature Innate Sorcery|Sorcerer|XPHB|1|XPHB}, when you fail a saving throw against a spell, you can choose to succeed instead.

subclassFeature.name: Hexblade Patron
entries: You've made a pact with a sentient magic weapon and the cursed forces contained within its blade. Such a weapon could be the sword sheathed at your side, or it could be a manifestation of an infamous magic weapon like {@item Blackrazor|XDMG} or the {@item Sword of Kas|XDMG} projecting its power to further its legend. To those willing to follow this weapon's whims, these inscrutable patrons offer the power to bestow malignant curses, deliver punishing blows, and bolster the wielder.

subclassFeature.name: Hexblade Spells
entries: The magic of your patron ensures you always have certain spells ready; when you reach a Warlock level specified in the {@table Hexblade Spells|ASRDR} table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

subclassFeature.name: Hexblade's Curse
entries: Your patron grants you the power to curse and hinder your foes. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself. The target is cursed for 1 minute, during which you gain the benefits below. The curse ends early if you use this feature again, if you dismiss it (no action required), or die.

You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. When you cast a spell using a spell slot that curses a target, you can use your Hexblade's Curse as a part of casting that spell instead of taking a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}. When you do so, the target of the spell is the target of your Hexblade's Curse, and your Hexblade's Curse's duration is either 1 minute or the spell's duration, whichever is longer.

entries.name: Hungering Hex
entries: When the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d8} plus your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Accursed Shield
entries: While you aren't wearing armor or wielding a {@item Shield|XPHB}, you gain a +2 bonus to AC while you are within 10 feet of the target cursed by your Hexblade's Curse.

subclassFeature.name: Unyielding Will
entries: When you succeed on a saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you takes {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage. Once you use this benefit, you can't do so again until the start of your next turn.

In addition, when you fail a saving throw to maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}, you can choose to succeed instead, and you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d10} plus your Warlock level. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Malign Brutality
entries: Your patron's might furthers your bloodthirst, granting you the following benefits.

entries.name: Harrowing Hex
entries: After you cast a level 1+ spell that has a casting time of an action, you can make one attack with a weapon as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

entries.name: Hindering Curse
entries: When you hit the target cursed by your {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} with an attack roll, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next saving throw it makes before the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Inescapable Hex
entries: When the target of your {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} ends its turn 30 feet or further from you, you can move up to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} straight toward the target.

subclassFeature.name: Armor of Hexes
entries: When you take damage from the target cursed by your {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR}, you can take a Reaction to reduce the damage taken by an amount equal to your Warlock level.

subclassFeature.name: Masterful Hex
entries: The power of your {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} increases, granting the following benefits.

entries.name: Accursed Critical
entries: Any attack roll you make against the target cursed by your {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} scores a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of a 19 or 20 on the d20.

entries.name: Explosive Hex
entries: When you deal damage to the target cursed by your {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR}, you can cause your curse to explode with sinister energy. The target and each creature of your choice in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the target take {@damage 3d6} Necrotic, Psychic, or Radiant damage (your choice), and their {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend a {@classFeature Pact Magic|Warlock|XPHB|1|XPHB} slot (no action required) to restore your use of it.

entries.name: Hex Restoration
entries: You regain one expended use of {@subclassFeature Hexblade's Curse|Warlock|XPHB|Hexblade|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} when you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB} or use your {@classFeature Magical Cunning|Warlock|XPHB|2|XPHB} feature.

subclassFeature.name: Conjuration Savant
entries: Choose two {@filter Wizard spells from the Conjuration school, each of which must be no higher than level 2|spells|level=0;1;2|class=wizard|school=c}, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Conjuration school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Focused Conjuration
entries: Taking damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on Conjuration spells.

subclassFeature.name: Quick Transposition
entries: You can use {@subclassFeature Benign Transposition|Wizard|XPHB|Conjurer|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

You can also use {@subclassFeature Benign Transposition|Wizard|XPHB|Conjurer|ASRDR|3|ASRDR} as a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} when a creature you can see makes an attack roll against you, but only to swap places with a willing creature. The creature that swaps places with you becomes the target of that attack instead.

subclassFeature.name: Enchanting Talker
entries: Whenever you make a Charisma check, you gain a bonus to the check equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of +1).
You also gain proficiency in one of these skills of your choice: {@skill Deception}, {@skill Intimidation}, or {@skill Persuasion}.

subclassFeature.name: Enchantment Savant
entries: Choose two {@filter Wizard spells from the Enchantment school, each of which must be no higher than level 2|spells|level=0;1;2|class=wizard|school=e}, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Enchantment school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Vexing Movement
entries: Immediately after you cast an Enchantment spell using an action and a spell slot, you can take both the {@action Disengage|XPHB} and {@action Dash|XPHB} actions as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

You can use this {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Reflecting Charm
entries: When a creature within 30 feet of you that you can see hits you with an attack roll, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to reduce the damage you take by half (round down), and you can force the attacker to make a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the attacker takes Psychic damage equal to half your Wizard level plus your Intelligence modifier. Once you use this {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, you can't do so again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. You can also restore your use of it by expending a level 2+ spell slot (no action required).

subclassFeature.name: Bolstering Belief
entries: Your enchantments fortify your allies' bodies and minds. You always have {@spell Power Word Fortify|XPHB} prepared. With this feature, you can cast the spell without expending a spell slot, and each target of the spell has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws to avoid or end the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} and {@condition Frightened|XPHB} conditions while it has {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} granted by this spell.
Once you cast the spell with this feature, you can't do so in this way again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

subclassFeature.name: Necromancy Savant
entries: Choose two {@filter Wizard spells from the Necromancy school, each of which must be no higher than level 2|spells|level=0;1;2|class=wizard|school=n}, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Necromancy school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Grave Power
entries: You have discovered more necromantic insights and inscribed them into your spellbook. While holding your spellbook, you gain the following benefits.

entries.name: Grave Resilience
entries: When you use {@classFeature Arcane Recovery|Wizard|XPHB|1|XPHB}, your {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level, if any, decreases by 1.

entries.name: Overwhelming Necrosis
entries: Damage from your Wizard spells and Wizard features ignores {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Necrotic damage.

subclassFeature.name: Undead Secrets
entries: You have learned more secrets about the nuances of life and death. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can expend a level 4+ spell slot to protect yourself from death. Until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, the next time you would drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead change to a number equal to ten times the spell slot expended.

In addition, immediately after you take damage and are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} after taking that damage but not killed outright, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} and teleport to an unoccupied space up to 60 feet from yourself, and each creature within 10 feet of the space you left takes {@damage 2d10} Necrotic damage.

subclassFeature.name: Bolster Undead
entries: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, choose any number of Undead you have created or summoned with a Necromancy spell that are within 60 feet of yourself. Those Undead each gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Wizard level. Once an Undead gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} from this feature, it can't gain them in this way again for the next 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Transmutation Savant
entries: Choose two {@filter Wizard spells from the Transmutation school, each of which must be no higher than level 2|spells|level=0;1;2|class=wizard|school=t}, and add them to your spellbook for free.

In addition, whenever you gain access to a new level of spell slots in this class, you can add one Wizard spell from the Transmutation school to your spellbook for free. The chosen spell must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

subclassFeature.name: Split Transmutation
entries: When you cast a Transmutation spell, such as {@spell Fly|XPHB}, that can be cast with a higher-level spell slot to target an additional creature, increase the spell's effective level by 1.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

race.name: Revenant
entries: A Revenant is a soul that has clawed its way back from the Shadowfell or the gates of the Fugue Plane to settle an unfinished score. Whether you were raised by a dark deity like The Raven Queen, fueled by the chaotic spite of Cyric, or kept alive by sheer force of will, your body is a vessel for singular purpose. You bear the scars of your first death—cold skin, pale eyes, or perhaps a visible wound that never heals—but your eyes burn with the terrifying light of a soul that refuses to depart.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are an Undead. You take damage by spells and magical effects that heal or restore life to Humanoids (such as {@spell cure wounds} or {@spell healing word}).

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small (depending on your form in life).

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Life Span
entries: You do not age. You remain in your current state until your purpose is fulfilled, at which point you crumble to dust.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You have Darkvision with a range of 60 feet.

entries.name: Relentless Nature
entries: You do not require air, food, drink, or sleep, and magic cannot put you to sleep. To gain the benefits of a Long Rest, you can spend 4 hours in an inactive, motionless state, during which you retain consciousness. You have advantage on saving throws against Exhaustion.

entries.name: Vengeful Tracker
entries: As a Bonus Action, you can designate one creature you can see or have a piece of (such as a lock of hair) as the target of your vengeance. For the next 24 hours, or until the target dies, you know the direction to the target as long as you are on the same plane of existence. Once per turn, when you hit that target with an attack, you deal extra Necrotic or Poison damage equal to your Proficiency Bonus. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all uses on a Long Rest.

entries.name: Vengeful Regeneration
entries: Your body knits itself back together with supernatural speed. When you have fewer than half your hit point maximum, you can use a Bonus Action to expend one of your Hit Dice, rolling it and regaining double that number of hit points. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, regaining all uses when you finish a Long or Short Rest.

entries.name: Deadly Tenacity
entries: Your proximity to death grants you a terrifying edge. You gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Death Throes:} You have advantage on death saving throws.

{@b Unstoppable Strike:} Once per turn, when you miss with an attack roll, you can choose to take Necrotic damage equal to twice your Proficiency Bonus (this damage cannot be reduced or prevented). If you do, you can add {@dice 1d6} to your attack roll, potentially turning the miss into a hit.

race.name: Reborn
entries: Death was not the end for you; you are a being of grafted flesh, alchemical fluids, or clockwork organs. You might remember bits and pieces of a previous life—a cold winter, a lover's face, the smell of ozone—but your current existence is defined by the strange power that keeps your patchwork form functioning.

entries.name: Creature Type
entries: You are both a Humanoid and a Construct. You are affected by an effect if it targets either of these creature types.

entries.name: Size
entries: You are Medium or Small (choose when you gain this species).

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your walking speed is 30 feet.

entries.name: Deathless Nature
entries: You have escaped the standard cycle of life and death. You gain the following benefits:

items: You have Advantage on saving throws against being Poisoned or Diseased.
You have Resistance to Poison damage.
You don't need to eat, drink, or breathe.
You have Advantage on Death Saving Throws.

entries.name: Ancestral Legacy
entries: You retain a fragment of your past life's prowess. You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice.

entries.name: Knowledge from a Past Life
entries: When you make an ability check or attack roll and fail, you can call upon your faded memories to help. You can roll {@dice 1d6} and add it to the total, potentially turning a failure into a success. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Proficiency Bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Synthetic Overdrive
entries: Your patchwork body can be pushed beyond its biological limits. As a Bonus Action, you can enter an Overdrive state for 1 minute. While in this state:

items: Your walking speed increases by 10 feet.
When you take Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage, you can use your Reaction to reduce the damage taken by an amount equal to your Proficiency Bonus.

entries: Once you use this feature, you cannot do so again until you finish a Long Rest.

entries.name: Restless Sentry
entries: You do not sleep. To gain the benefits of a Long Rest, you enter a motionless state for 4 hours. In this state, you appear inert, but it doesn't render you {@condition Unconscious}, and you can see and hear as normal.

spell.name: Chains of Karsus

components.m: a cold iron chain link and a shard of a shattered spellcasting focus

entries: You summon ethereal chains of raw, crackling anti-magic—born from the residual arrogance of an ancient archmage—to bind a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or become {@condition restrained} by the magical chains for the duration.
While {@condition restrained} by this spell, the target suffers the following effects:

items: {@b Arcane Siphon.} The target takes {@damage 4d8} force damage at the start of each of its turns as the chains violently tear at its life force and magical essence.

{@b Weave Disruption.} The chains actively suppress the target's ability to manipulate magic. If the target attempts to cast a spell, it must first succeed on a spellcasting ability check against your spell save DC. On a failed check, the spell fails and the action (or bonus action or reaction) is wasted, though the spell slot is not consumed.
{@b Broken Concentration.} The target has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws made to maintain concentration on a spell.

entries: A creature {@condition restrained} by the chains can use its action to make a Strength or Dexterity check (its choice) against your spell save DC. On a success, it frees itself and the spell ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the force damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Dethrone
entries: You shred a foe's very essence by pulling on strands of the Weave, channeling the corrosive force of stolen divinity. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw. It takes {@damage 10d6+20} necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.
If the target is a Celestial, this spell automatically deals maximum damage (80 necrotic damage) regardless of the saving throw result, and the target has disadvantage on its saving throw.

spell.name: Curriculum of Strategy: Artistry of War
entries: You summon the apparitions of 6 master strategists. Each apparition strikes a target of your choosing within range, automatically dealing {@damage 2d6+6} force damage (for a total of {@damage 12d6+36} force damage). The projectiles home unerringly and cannot be blocked by the {@spell shield} spell.
You may distribute the strikes among any number of targets you can see within range.

spell.name: Elminster's Fakeout

components.m: a pinch of ash from a wizard's pipe and a cracked glass eye

entries: You touch a willing creature, placing a highly deceptive ward upon them. The first time the target would drop to 0 hit points as a result of taking damage, the ward triggers and the spell ends.
When the ward triggers, the target drops to 0 hit points but is immediately stabilized. Simultaneously, the following effects occur:

items: {@b The Decoy.} A perfect, lifeless illusion of the target's corpse falls to the ground in the space they just occupied, complete with convincing battle damage. The illusion lasts for 1 minute or until a creature uses an action to examine it and succeeds on an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check against your spell save DC.
{@b The Escape.} The actual target becomes {@condition invisible} and instantly teleports to an unoccupied space of their choice that they can see within 30 feet.
{@b The Buffer.} The target gains {@dice 5d10} temporary hit points.

entries: The target remains {@condition unconscious} as long as they are at 0 hit points. The invisibility lasts for 1 minute, or until the target regains any actual hit points. While the target has these temporary hit points, any damage they take is absorbed by the temporary hit points first, preventing them from suffering a death saving throw failure (unless the incoming damage completely depletes the temporary hit points and damages their actual hit points).

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the temporary hit points increase by {@dice 1d10} for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Ironbound Rush
entries: You rush forward up to your maximum movement speed in a straight line, reinforcing yourself with metal. This movement stops early if you come in contact with a surface or creature. If you come in contact with a creature, they must succeed on a Strength saving throw or take {@damage 3d6} bludgeoning damage and be knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage and is not knocked prone.

entries.name: Ironclad
entries: If you are wearing metal armor, the damage becomes {@damage 3d10}, and you can choose to push back the target up to 15 feet in a straight line instead of knocking them prone.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the distance you move increases by 10 feet for each spell slot above the 2nd, and the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} (or {@dice 1d10}) for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Moonflare
entries: You launch a pulse of radiant energy at a target within range. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} radiant damage. On a critical hit, the target is covered in glittering silver light, granting advantage on attack rolls against the target until the end of your next turn.

Alternatively, you can launch the pulse at a point in the air within range where it slowly falls towards the ground. Until the end of your next turn, the flare sheds bright silvery light in a 20-foot radius and dim light for an additional 20 feet.

This spell's damage increases by {@dice 1d8}, and the radius of the bright and dim light increases by 10 feet, when you reach 5th level ({@damage 2d8}, 30 feet), 11th level ({@damage 3d8}, 40 feet), and 17th level ({@damage 4d8}, 50 feet).

spell.name: Moonless Sky
entries: You transform the ground in a 45-foot-radius, 20-foot-high cylinder centered on a point you can see within range into a breathtaking, illusory void of the night sky filled with swirling nebulae and glittering constellations.

For your enemies, the floor appears to completely vanish into a bottomless cosmic abyss. The area is difficult terrain for your enemies. When an enemy enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, that creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or gain the {@condition prone} condition.
While you are within the area, you are bathed in a shifting, ethereal starlight that enhances your movements. You gain the following benefits while inside the area:

items: {@b Stellar Glide.} Your speed increases by 15 feet, and you ignore difficult terrain.
{@b Astral Dance.} You can take the Disengage action as a Bonus Action.
{@b Moonlit Radiance.} You shed Dim Light in a 10-foot radius. Attack rolls against you have Disadvantage as your form blurs and twinkles against the backdrop of the stars.

spell.name: Bloodthorns

components.m: a dried briar dipped in blood

entries: Vines of hardened, crystalline crimson blood erupt and wrap around your arms and weapons. Until the spell ends, your reach for melee attacks increases by 5 feet.
In addition, once per turn, you can cause the thorns to violently lash out when one of the following triggers occurs:

items: {@b When You Hit.} You hit a creature with a melee attack.
{@b When You Are Hit.} A creature within 5 feet of you hits you with a melee attack.

entries: When the thorns lash out, the target takes {@damage 2d6} Piercing damage and {@damage 2d6} Necrotic damage, and its speed is reduced by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.
Whenever a creature takes damage from this spell you gain Temporary Hit Points equal to the Necrotic damage the target took from this manifestation.

subclassFluff.name: Magic Stealer
entries: {@i Steal Magical Power to Empower Yourself}

subclassFluff.name: Warrior of the Mystic Arts
entries: {@i Weave Martial and Mystic Arts}

subclassFluff.name: Arcane Archer
entries: {@i Deploy Magical Effects through Enchanted Ammunition}

entries.name: Design Note: Arcane Archer Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in {@class Fighter|XPHB|Unearthed Arcana|Arcane Archer|ASRDR}:

items.name: Curving Shot
entries: has been revised to use the same ammunition but a new attack roll.

items.name: Magical Ammunition
entries: is a new feature that grants magical effects to your ammunition that can be used outside of combat.

items.name: Ever-Ready Shot
entries: is now available at a later level but always regains one expended Arcane Shot Die.

items.name: Arcane Burst
entries: is a new feature that grants an additional effect to {@classFeature Indomitable|Fighter|XPHB|9|XPHB}.

items.name: Masterful Shots
entries: has been redesigned and now allows you to reposition and attack.

subclassFluff.name: Tattooed Warrior
entries: {@i Enhance Martial Arts with Your Magic Tattoos}

entries.name: Design Note: Tattooed Warrior Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its last appearance in {@class Monk|XPHB|Unearthed Arcana|Tattooed Warrior|ASRDR}:

items: The Tattooed Warrior has been redesigned. With some exceptions, the tattoos now focus on additional abilities instead of spells.

items.name: Beast Tattoos
entries: still grant a cantrip, but now each option provides a benefit that isn't tied to a level 1 spell.

items.name: Celestial Tattoo
entries: has been redesigned to provide uses of Focus Points outside of combat.

items.name: Nature Tattoo
entries: has been condensed to provide Damage {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to a small suite of choices that can change.

items.name: Monster Tattoos
entries: has been redesigned with new options.

subclassFluff.name: Ancestral Sorcery
entries: {@i Bear the Power of a Spellcasting Lineage}

subclassFluff.name: Arcana Domain
entries: {@i Blend Conviction with Arcane Power}

entries.name: Design Note: Arcana Domain Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in the {@book Sword Coast Adventurer's Guide|SCAG}:

items.name: Arcane Domain Spells
entries: have been revised.

items.name: Arcane Initiate
entries: now gives you {@variantrule Expertise|XPHB} in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill.

items.name: Modify Magic
entries: is a new feature that replaces {@subclassFeature Channel Divinity: Arcane Abjuration|Cleric|PHB|Arcana|SCAG|2|SCAG|Arcane Abjuration}.

items.name: Dispelling Recovery
entries: (formerly named {@subclassFeature Spell Breaker|Cleric|PHB|Arcana|SCAG|6|SCAG}) now uses {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} and also triggers when you remove a condition from a creature.

items.name: Arcane Mastery
entries: now lets you change the {@class Wizard|XPHB} spells you choose.

subclassFluff.name: Conjurer
entries: {@i Step Across Space and Call Creatures from Thin Air}

entries.name: Design Note: Conjurer Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in the {@book 2014 Player's Handbook|PHB}:

items.name: Benign Transposition
entries: is now available at a lower level, and the new {@subclassFeature Distant Transposition|Wizard|XPHB|Conjurer|ASRDR|6|ASRDR} and {@subclassFeature Quick Transposition|Wizard|XPHB|Conjurer|ASRDR|14|ASRDR|Quick Teleportation} features improve it.

items.name: Conjuration Savant
entries: now gives you free Conjuration spells to add to your spellbook.

items.name: Durable Summons
entries: is now available at a lower level, and the {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} it grants are based on your Wizard level.

subclassFluff.name: Enchanter
entries: {@i Entrance and Beguile Others}

entries.name: Design Note: Enchanter Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in the {@book 2014 Player's Handbook|PHB}:

items.name: Enchantment Savant
entries: now gives you free Enchantment spells to add to your spellbook.

items.name: Enchanting Talker and Vexing Movement
entries: are new features that replace {@subclassFeature Hypnotic Gaze|Wizard|PHB|Enchantment|PHB|2|PHB}, giving the Wizard new options to use in and out of combat.

items.name: Reflecting Charm
entries: replaces the former {@subclassFeature Instinctive Charm|Wizard|PHB|Enchantment|PHB|6|PHB} feature; instead of compelling an attacker to choose a new target, the feature now reliably reduces the damage you take and might harm the attacker.

items.name: Bolstering Belief
entries: is a new feature to aid yourself and allies in many situations.

subclassFluff.name: Hexblade Patron
entries: {@i Forge a Pact with a Cursed Blade}

entries.name: Design Note: Hexblade Patron Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its {@class Warlock|XPHB|latest appearance in Unearthed Arcana|Hexblade|XUA2025HorrorSubclasses}:

items.name: Hexblade Spells
entries: have been revised.

items.name: Hexblade's Curse
entries: has returned as a feature in its own right but can also loop through spells that place a curse on a creature, such as {@spell Bestow Curse|XPHB} and {@spell Hex|XPHB}. Hungering Hex has been moved to this level, and Accursed Shield is a new feature.

items.name: Unyielding Will
entries: is a new feature that grants you benefits when maintaining {@status Concentration|XPHB} on spells.

items.name: Malign Brutality
entries: is a new feature that gives you additional benefits. Hindering Curse (formerly the Stymying Mark maneuver) has been moved here.

items.name: Armor of Hexes
entries: no longer has limited uses and scales with your Warlock level.

items.name: Masterful Hex
entries: now increases the power and usability of the updated Hexblade's Curse feature.

subclassFluff.name: Necromancer
entries: {@i Command the Powers of Death and Undeath}

entries.name: Design Note: Necromancer Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in the {@book 2014 Player's Handbook|PHB}:

items.name: Necromancy Savant
entries: now gives you free Necromancy spells to add to your spellbook.

items.name: Necromancy Spellbook
entries: is a new feature that incorporates a redesigned version of the prior {@subclassFeature Grim Harvest|Wizard|PHB|Necromancy|PHB|2|PHB} feature.

items.name: Grave Power, Undead Secrets, and Death's Master
entries: are new features.

items.name: Undead Thralls
entries: has been redesigned to utilize the {@spell Summon Undead|XPHB} spell.

subclassFluff.name: Transmuter
entries: {@i Transform Energy and Matter}

entries.name: Design Note: Transmuter Updates
entries: Here are the main updates in this subclass since its appearance in the {@book 2014 Player's Handbook|PHB}:

items.name: Transmutation Savant
entries: now gives you free Transmutation spells to add to your spellbook.

items.name: Transmuter's Stone
entries: is now available at a lower level. You create the stone when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

items.name: Wondrous Enhancement and Split Transmutation
entries: are new features.

items.name: Potent Stone
entries: is a new feature that gives you more options with your transmuter's stone.

items.name: Master Transmuter
entries: is revised; its Panacea choice now restores {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} and can remove a curse.

table.name: Arcana Domain Spells
rows: {@spell Detect Magic|XPHB}, {@spell Magic Missile|XPHB}, {@spell Magic Weapon|XPHB}, {@spell Nystul's Magic Aura|XPHB}

table.name: Hexblade Spells
rows: {@spell Arcane Vigor|XPHB}, {@spell Hex|XPHB}, {@spell Shield|XPHB}, {@spell Wrathful Smite|XPHB}

table.name: Lineage Spells
rows: {@spell Command|XPHB}, {@spell Guidance|XPHB}, {@spell Locate Object|XPHB}, {@spell Protection from Evil and Good|XPHB}, {@spell Resistance|XPHB}, {@spell Spiritual Weapon|XPHB}

data.name: Unearthed Arcana 2026: Mystic Subclasses
entries: This playtest document is part of a series of {@i Unearthed Arcana} articles that present material designed for upcoming products. The material here uses the rules in the {@book 2024 Player's Handbook|XPHB}.

entries.name: What's Inside
entries: This document presents four new subclasses:

items: {@class Monk} ({@class Monk|XPHB|Warrior of the Mystic Arts|Mystic Arts|XUA2026MysticSubclasses})
{@class Paladin} ({@class Paladin|XPHB|Oath of the Spellguard|Spellguard|XUA2026MysticSubclasses})
{@class Rogue} ({@class Rogue|XPHB|Magic Stealer|Magic Stealer|XUA2026MysticSubclasses})
{@class Warlock} ({@class Warlock|XPHB|Vestige Patron|Vestige|XUA2026MysticSubclasses})

entries.name: This Is Playtest Material
entries: This article is presented for playtesting and feedback. The options here are experimental and in draft form. They aren't officially part of the game. Your feedback will help determine whether we adopt it as official.

items.name: How to Playtest This UA
entries: We invite you to try out this material in play. To play with this material, you may either incorporate it into your campaign or run one or more special playtest sessions. For such a session, you may create an adventure of your own or use a short adventure from a source like {@adventure Dragon Delves|DrDe-BD}.

items.name: Power Level
entries: The character options you read here might be more or less powerful than options in the {@book 2024 Player's Handbook|XPHB}. If a design survives playtesting, we adjust its power to the desirable level before publication. This means an option could be more or less powerful in its final form.

items.name: Feedback
entries: The best way for you to give us feedback on this material is in the survey we'll release on D&D Beyond. If we make this material official, it will be refined based on your feedback, and then it will appear in a D&D book.
Providing feedback on this document is one way you can help shape the future of D&D!

entries.name: Warrior of the Mystic Arts (Monk)
entries: {@i Weave Martial and Mystic Arts}

entries.name: Oath of the Spellguard (Paladin)
entries: {@i Protect Your Allies from Villainous Magic}

entries.name: Magic Stealer (Rogue)
entries: {@i Steal Magical Power to Empower Yourself}

entries.name: Vestige Patron (Warlock)
entries: {@i Wield the Remnants of a Dying God's Power}

optionalfeature.name: Banishing Shot
entries: Your ammunition temporarily sequesters your target in a harmless demiplane. The creature you hit takes additional Psychic damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be banished. While banished, the creature has the {@condition Incapacitated} condition and a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0. At the end of its next turn, the target reappears in the space it left or, if that space is occupied, in the nearest unoccupied space.

optionalfeature.name: Bat
entries: You know the {@spell Dancing Lights|XPHB} cantrip. You also gain {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} with a range of 10 feet.

optionalfeature.name: Beguiling Shot
entries: Your ammunition beguiles your target. The creature you hit takes additional Psychic damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed} condition until the start of your next turn, treating either you or one of your allies within 30 feet of the target (your choice) as the charmer. The {@condition Charmed} condition ends early if the charmer attacks the target, deals damage to it, or forces it to make a saving throw.

optionalfeature.name: Beholder
entries: At the start of your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to gain a {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB} equal to your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} for 10 minutes. While you have this {@variantrule Fly Speed|XPHB}, you can hover.

Additionally, as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to fire four rays from your eyes. You can fire them at one target you can see within 120 feet or at several. Make a ranged spell attack for each ray, using Wisdom as your spellcasting ability. On a hit, the attack deals Force damage equal to one roll of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier.

optionalfeature.name: Butterfly
entries: You know the {@spell Light|XPHB} cantrip. When you make a {@variantrule High Jump|XPHB}, you can use your Dexterity modifier instead of your Strength modifier to determine how high you can jump.

optionalfeature.name: Chromatic Dragon
entries: When you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action on your turn, you can expend 1 Focus Point to replace one of your attacks with an exhalation of magical energy in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Choose a damage type: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. Each creature in that area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage of the chosen type equal to two rolls of your Martial Arts die plus your Wisdom modifier. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

optionalfeature.name: Comet
entries: When you take the {@action Search|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to roll your Martial Arts die and add the number rolled to the Wisdom check.

optionalfeature.name: Crane
entries: You know the {@spell Guidance|XPHB} cantrip. When you miss a creature with an attack granted by your {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on your next attack roll against that creature before the end of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Displacer Beast
entries: When you expend a Focus Point to use {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB} or {@classFeature Step of the Wind|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, you can expend 1 Focus Point to cast the {@spell Mirror Image|XPHB} spell as part of that {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Eclipse
entries: When you take the {@action Hide|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to roll your Martial Arts die and add the number rolled to the Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) check.

optionalfeature.name: Grasping Shot
entries: Your ammunition creates clutching brambles around your target. The creature you hit takes additional Slashing damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die and must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Restrained} condition for 1 minute or until you use this option again. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your Arcane Shot DC, removing the brambles and ending the {@condition Restrained} condition on the target on a successful check.

optionalfeature.name: Horse
entries: You know the {@spell Message|XPHB} cantrip. When you expend 1 Focus Point to use {@classFeature Step of the Wind|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Sea Storm
entries: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Cold, Lightning, or Thunder. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, or use your {@classFeature Uncanny Metabolism|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB} feature, you can change this choice.

optionalfeature.name: Seeking Shot
entries: Your ammunition can seek out a target. When you use this option, you don't make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, choose one creature you have seen in the last minute. The ammunition flies toward that creature, moving around corners if necessary and ignoring {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover} and {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}. If the target is within your weapon's long range, the target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes damage as if it were hit plus additional Force damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die, and you learn the target's current location. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only. If the target is beyond your weapon's long range, the ammunition disappears after traveling as far as it can.

optionalfeature.name: Shadow Shot
entries: Your ammunition occludes your foe's vision with shadows. The creature you hit takes additional Psychic damage equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die, and it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Sunburst
entries: When you take the {@action Study|XPHB} action, you can expend 1 Focus Point to roll your Martial Arts die and add the number rolled to the Intelligence check.

optionalfeature.name: Tortoise
entries: You know the {@spell Spare the Dying|XPHB} cantrip. When you expend 1 Focus Point to use {@classFeature Patient Defense|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, you have a +1 bonus to AC until the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Troll
entries: At the start of each of your turns, you regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to 5 plus your Wisdom modifier if you are {@status Bloodied|XPHB} and have at least 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}. Any severed body part regrows after you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Volcano
entries: You gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to one of the following damage types of your choice: Acid, Fire, or Poison. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, or use your {@classFeature Uncanny Metabolism|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB} feature, you can change this choice.

optionalfeature.name: Blink Dog
entries: When you expend a Focus Point to use {@classFeature Patient Defense|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, you can expend 3 Focus Points to cast {@spell Blink|XPHB} immediately after that {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Bursting Shot
entries: You imbue your ammunition with explosive force energy. Immediately after you deal damage to the creature, your target and each creature within a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from the target takes Force damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die.

optionalfeature.name: Chameleon
entries: You know the {@spell Minor Illusion|XPHB} cantrip. You can expend 1 Focus Point to cast {@spell Disguise Self|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Crescent Moon
entries: You can expend 2 Focus Points to cast {@spell Misty Step|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Enfeebling Shot
entries: Your ammunition saps your target's strength. The creature you hit takes additional Necrotic damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die. The target must also succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Poisoned} condition until the end of its next turn. Whenever a target {@condition Poisoned} in this way hits with an attack roll, it subtracts an amount equal to one roll of your Arcane Shot Die from the total damage of that attack.

optionalfeature.name: Guardian Naga
entries: When you would be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} but not killed outright, your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} instead change to a number equal to twice your Monk level. Once you use this benefit, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

optionalfeature.name: Mountain
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 3 Focus Points to gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Acid damage and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws for 1 minute.

optionalfeature.name: Piercing Shot
entries: You give your ammunition an ethereal quality. When you use this option, you don't make an attack roll for the attack. Instead, the ammunition shoots forward in a 30-foot {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} that is 1 foot wide, originating from you, then vanishes. The {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} ignores cover, as the ammunition phases through solid objects. Each creature in the {@variantrule Line [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Line} must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes damage as if it were hit plus additional Piercing damage equal to two rolls of your Arcane Shot Die. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage.

optionalfeature.name: Spider
entries: You know the {@spell Mending|XPHB} cantrip. When you hit a creature with an attack granted by your {@classFeature Flurry of Blows|Monk|XPHB|2|XPHB}, the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.

optionalfeature.name: Storm
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 3 Focus Points to gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Lightning damage and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Dexterity saving throws for 1 minute.

optionalfeature.name: Wave
entries: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can expend 3 Focus Points to gain {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to Cold damage and {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Wisdom saving throws for 1 minute.

feat.name: Ricochet Acrobat
entries: You have mastered the art of using your strikes and momentum to vault off targets and launch yourself into spectacular aerial maneuvers. You gain the following benefits:

items: {@b Ability Score Increase.} Increase your Strength or Dexterity score by 1, to a maximum of 20.

{@b Wall Spring.} Immediately after you hit a creature with a melee attack, or immediately after you take the Dash or Disengage action, you can move across vertical surfaces (like walls or pillars) as if they were flat ground until the end of the current turn. If you end your turn on a vertical surface, you fall.

{@b Death from Above.} You can channel your downward momentum into a crushing blow. If you leap or fall at least 10 feet vertically downward immediately before hitting a creature with a melee weapon attack, you have Advantage on the attack roll. If the attack hits, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC 8 + your Proficiency Bonus + the ability modifier used for the attack) or gain the {@condition prone} condition.
{@b Break the Fall.} You can use your Reaction when you fall to reduce the damage taken from the fall by an amount equal to five times your Proficiency Bonus.
